consent.banner: Optional and anonymous — these are reflection prompts, not a diagnosis. Your answers are never stored or tied to you.
consent.footer: For curiosity, not diagnosis. Nothing here infers medical conditions or political beliefs, and no personal profile is ever built.

markers.name: The Mirror
markers.short: Scoring your inside against everyone else's outside.
markers.interpretation: We measure ourselves against others when no objective yardstick exists — feeds weaponize it by pairing your ordinary Tuesday with everyone else's highlight reel, forever.

deeper.q: Compared your week to someone's post this week?
resp: You compared your bloopers to their trailer. Rigged game — score your own tape instead.
Keep scoring your own tape. That's the whole cheat code.

markers.name: The Lever
markers.short: The slot-machine schedule inside every pull-to-refresh.
markers.interpretation: Feeds pay out on a variable-ratio schedule — the same reinforcement pattern as a slot machine. It isn't the reward that hooks you, it's the maybe.

deeper.q: Refreshed a feed today expecting nothing new?
resp: That's the lever. The casino fits in a pocket now — noticing the pull is how you loosen it.
Noticing the lever is the only real cheat code. Keep it.

markers.name: The Reflex
markers.short: Reaching for the phone before deciding to.
markers.interpretation: Checking becomes a motor habit — cue, reach, unlock — that runs before conscious choice gets a vote. The behavior asks permission afterward, if at all.

deeper.q: Ever unlocked your phone and forgotten why?
resp: That's the reflex running without you. Putting one step between cue and reach — a lock screen question, a different pocket — gives choice its vote back.
Then your hands still work for you. Guard that.

markers.name: The Counter
markers.short: Outsourcing your mood to a number under a post.
markers.interpretation: When approval arrives as a count, the count starts standing in for the approval — and your baseline mood quietly gets indexed to it.

deeper.q: Posted something and checked back within ten minutes?
resp: The check-back is the tell — the post was partly a question. Nothing wrong with wanting an answer; just notice who you gave the scoreboard to.
Post-and-walk is the strongest posture on the internet.

markers.name: The Low Battery
markers.short: Drained by connection that never lands.
markers.interpretation: A hundred shallow interactions can spend the same energy as one deep one — without returning any of it. The battery reads empty in a full room.

deeper.q: After an hour of scrolling, do you feel more connected or more tired?
resp: That tiredness is data: the feed spends social energy without recharging it. One real conversation pays back more than a hundred taps.
Then you're using it, not the other way around.

markers.name: The Detour
markers.short: The scroll as a swerve around something harder.
markers.interpretation: Sometimes the phone isn't the destination — it's the detour around a feeling, a task, or a conversation that's waiting. The route feels easier; the thing stays waiting.

deeper.q: Last time you opened an app — moving toward something, or away from something?
resp: Naming what you swerved around is half the trip back. The waiting thing gets smaller when you look at it.
Intentional use is the rarest kind. Respect.

markers.name: The Climb
markers.short: Wanting the glow-up — fuel, when it's pointed right.
markers.interpretation: Watching others rise can burn as envy or as fuel. Same fire, different furnace — the difference is whether it moves your feet or just your feed.

deeper.q: Someone's win last week — did it move you to act, or just to watch more?
resp: That's drive doing its actual job. Point it at one rep today.
Watching is research for a while, then it's a residence. Pick one thing theirs proves is possible.

markers.name: The Gauge
markers.short: Life read as a scoreboard that never sleeps.
markers.interpretation: When everything is measurable — views, streaks, response times — everything starts to feel graded. The gauge is useful until it's the whole dashboard.

deeper.q: Does going quiet online for a week feel like falling behind?
resp: That's the gauge talking, not the work. The work survives silence; the metrics were never the mission.
You've kept the gauge in its lane. That's rare.

markers.name: The Double
markers.short: The online you and the offline you, drifting apart.
markers.interpretation: Everyone edits. The marker isn't having a public self — it's when the distance between the profile and the person gets wide enough that both feel like costumes.

deeper.q: Would the people who know you offline recognize your feed?
resp: A gap that wide costs energy to maintain — closing it even a little returns the deposit.
One self, both places. That's the cheapest way to live.

markers.name: The Polish
markers.short: Curating the shot instead of living the moment.
markers.interpretation: Impression management is older than the internet — but a camera in every pocket turned it into a part-time job. The polish protects the image and taxes the moment.

deeper.q: Ever re-shot a casual photo more than three times?
resp: The retakes are the tell: the audience entered the room. Some moments deserve to stay unphotographed.
One-take living. The feed can keep up or catch up.

markers.name: The Stall
markers.short: Trading tomorrow's goal for tonight's relief.
markers.interpretation: Procrastination isn't laziness — it's mood repair. The task feels bad, the app feels better, and the trade repeats until the deadline collects.

deeper.q: You keep saying you'll make the content. Made any this week?
resp: The loop breaks on contact: one ugly first version tonight beats the perfect one that stays imaginary.
Shipping is the exit. You found the door.

markers.name: The Funnel
markers.short: Only letting in what you already agree with.
markers.interpretation: We hunt for evidence we were already right — and feeds learn the hunt, then feed it. The funnel feels like clarity; it's actually a shrinking aperture.

deeper.q: When did you last change your mind because of something you read?
resp: If nothing gets through, the funnel's doing the reading for you. Follow one smart voice you disagree with.
A mind that still moves is the whole point of having one.

markers.name: The Switch
markers.short: Turning what you notice into what you do.
markers.interpretation: Attention is the raw material; action is the product. The switch flips when noticing something — a skill, a gap, an idea — converts into a rep instead of another saved post.

deeper.q: Your saved folder: launchpad or graveyard?
resp: Saving feels like doing — that's the trap. Open one saved thing today and act on it for ten minutes.
You've built the switch. Most people never wire it.

markers.name: The Line
markers.short: Knowing where you end and the feed begins.
markers.interpretation: A boundary is a signal, not a wall — hours the phone doesn't get, rooms it doesn't enter, questions it doesn't answer. The line protects the person drawing it.

deeper.q: Is there any hour of your day the phone reliably doesn't get?
resp: Start with one — the first hour, the last hour, or the meal. One kept line teaches the rest.
One kept line teaches the rest. You already know.

markers.name: The Frame
markers.short: Noticing when the room was built to move you.
markers.interpretation: Every pitch chooses a frame — what's compared, what's hidden, what's urgent. Seeing the frame doesn't make you cynical; it makes you the one person in the room who chose.

deeper.q: Last thing you bought online — did you find it, or did it find you?
resp: Nothing wrong with being found — just know the introduction was engineered. Ask who built the frame.
Buyer on offense. That's the strongest position in the market.

markers.name: The Hourglass
markers.short: Urgency and FOMO doing the selling.
markers.interpretation: 'Only 2 left' and 'everyone's going' work on anticipated regret — the feed shows departures, never the delays. An anxious buyer checks back sooner; that's the business model.

deeper.q: Seen a trip this month you 'should' have been on?
resp: The window seat looks better than it flies. Real scarcity is rare; manufactured scarcity is a Tuesday.
Movement without the itch — that's the actual flex.

markers.name: The One-Way Wire
markers.short: Knowing a voice that doesn't know you exist.
markers.interpretation: Parasocial bonds — one-way relationships with creators and hosts — can comfort, and can also quietly substitute for the two-way kind that costs and returns more.

deeper.q: Is there a creator you'd say you 'know'?
resp: If it sounds like you, I guess I really know you — that line is this feeling. Spend one hour of that loyalty on someone who can say your name back.
Two-way only. Old school, still undefeated.

markers.name: The Crowded Room
markers.interpretation: The loneliness paradox: heavy connectivity tracks with feeling less connected. A thousand weak ties can't do the work of one strong one.

deeper.q: If tonight went sideways, how many contacts could you actually call?
resp: The number matters less than the trend — one real call this week starts rebuilding the beam.
Load-bearing people. That's wealth the feed can't display.

markers.name: The Watcher
markers.short: Thinking about your own thinking — the master key.
markers.interpretation: Metacognition is the skill over all the others: noticing the lever, the mirror, the detour while they happen. Every other marker on this page is easier once the watcher is awake.

deeper.q: Reading this right now — is that the watcher waking up?
resp: That noticing is the muscle. Everything on this page gets easier from here.
Then you've been running the room all along. Own it — only way to beat it.
